n. The common designation of one a sect founded by the Rev.
   Edward Irving (about 1830), who call themselves the Catholic Apostolic
   Church. They are highly ritualistic in worship, have an elaborate
   hierarchy of apostles, prophets, etc., and look for the speedy coming
   of Christ.
